<skill_content name="sense-conventions">
# Skill: sense-conventions

# Check conventions with Sense

Use Sense conventions to understand project patterns before writing new code.

## Steps

1. Run `sense_conventions` to see all detected patterns.
2. If working in a specific domain, run `sense_conventions domain="<domain>"` to filter.
3. Follow the detected patterns in your new code — match naming conventions, file structure, testing patterns, and inheritance hierarchies.
4. If you need to deviate from a convention, note why.

Base directory for this skill: file:///Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/sense/wagtail/.opencode/skills/sense-conventions
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>

289: class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
290:     title = models.CharField(
291:         verbose_name=_("title"),
292:         max_length=255,
293:         help_text=_("The page title as you'd like it to be seen by the public"),
294:     )
295:     title.required_on_save = True
296:     # to reflect title of a current draft in the admin UI
297:     draft_title = models.CharField(max_length=255, editable=False)
298:     slug = models.SlugField(
299:         verbose_name=_("slug"),
300:         allow_unicode=True,
301:         max_length=255,
302:         help_text=_(
303:             "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
304:         ),
305:     )
306:     content_type = models.ForeignKey(
307:         ContentType,
308:         verbose_name=_("content type"),
309:         related_name="pages",
310:         on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
311:     )
312:     content_type.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
313:     url_path = models.TextField(verbose_name=_("URL path"), blank=True, editable=False)
314:     owner = models.ForeignKey(
315:         settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
316:         verbose_name=_("owner"),
317:         null=True,
318:         blank=True,
319:         editable=True,
320:         on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
321:         related_name="owned_pages",
322:     )
323:     owner.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
324: 
325:     seo_title = models.CharField(
326:         verbose_name=_("title tag"),
327:         max_length=255,
328:         blank=True,
329:         help_text=_(
330:             "The name of the page displayed on search engine results as the clickable headline."
331:         ),
332:     )
333: 
334:     show_in_menus_default = False
335:     show_in_menus = models.BooleanField(
336:         verbose_name=_("show in menus"),
337:         default=False,
338:         help_text=_(
339:             "Whether a link to this page will appear in automatically generated menus"
340:         ),
341:     )
342:     search_description = models.TextField(
343:         verbose_name=_("meta description"),
344:         blank=True,
345:         help_text=_(
346:             "The descriptive text displayed underneath a headline in search engine results."
347:         ),
348:     )
349: 
350:     latest_revision_created_at = models.DateTimeField(
351:         verbose_name=_("latest revision created at"), null=True, editable=False
352:     )
353: 
354:     _revisions = GenericRelation(
355:         "wagtailcore.Revision",
356:         content_type_field="content_type",
357:         object_id_field="object_id",
358:         related_query_name="page",
359:         for_concrete_model=False,
360:     )
361: 
362:     # Override WorkflowMixin's GenericRelation to specify related_query_name
363:     # so we can do WorkflowState.objects.filter(page=...) queries.
364:     # There is no need to override the workflow_states property, as the default
365:     # implementation in WorkflowMixin already ensures that the queryset uses the
366:     # base Page content type.
367:     _workflow_states = GenericRelation(
368:         "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
369:         content_type_field="base_content_type",
370:         object_id_field="object_id",
371:         related_query_name="page",
372:         for_concrete_model=False,
373:     )
374: 
375:     # When using a specific queryset, accessing the _workflow_states GenericRelation
376:     # will yield no results. This is because the _workflow_states GenericRelation
377:     # uses the base_content_type as the content_type_field, which is not the same
378:     # as the content type of the specific queryset. To work around this, we define
379:     # a second GenericRelation that uses the specific content_type to be used
380:     # when working with specific querysets.
381:     _specific_workflow_states = GenericRelation(
382:         "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
383:         content_type_field="content_type",
384:         object_id_field="object_id",
385:         related_query_name="page",
386:         for_concrete_model=False,
387:     )
388:
